disclaimer: Entry rules can change. Always verify with official immigration or embassy sources before booking.

visa.notes: Australian passport holders can obtain a Visitor Visa on Arrival (VOA) or e-VOA at major international airports and seaports including Bali (DPS), Jakarta (CGK) and Lombok. Fee is IDR 500,000 (approx. USD 30 / €29), payable by card or cash. Extendable once at a local immigration office for 30 additional days. Use only the official Indonesian immigration site to avoid scam visa services.
visa.verify_note: Indonesia's visa rules and fees can change. Check molina.imigrasi.go.id or the Indonesian embassy before travel.

insurance.notes: Strongly recommended. Medical facilities outside Bali and Jakarta are limited. Surfing, scooter riding and volcano trekking are high-risk — ensure your policy covers these explicitly. Medical evacuation from remote islands can be very costly.

regions.notes: Dry season May–Oct. Peak tourist season Jul–Aug. Shoulder May–Jun and Sep–Oct is ideal. Rainy season Nov–Apr.
Sunrise at Mt Bromo is best in dry season when skies are clear.
Komodo diving is best Apr–Nov. Rough seas in wet season can cancel boat trips.
Unusual pattern — heavy swells Jul–Aug. Diving is world-class Oct–Apr.

budget.verify_note: IDR rate can fluctuate. Check xe.com before travel. Always use official money changers in Bali — counterfeit deals exist.

budget.label: Backpacker
budget.includes: Dorm bed or cheap guesthouse, warungs 3x, local transport, 1 Bintang

mid.label: Comfort
mid.includes: Private guesthouse or mid hotel, restaurants, scooter rental, activities

useful_apps.name: Gojek
useful_apps.use: Ride-hailing, food delivery — dominant on Java. Also covers Bali.

useful_apps.name: Grab
useful_apps.use: Ride-hailing — works in major cities and Bali

useful_apps.name: Google Maps
useful_apps.use: Navigation — download offline maps, especially outside cities

useful_apps.name: Traveloka
useful_apps.use: Flights, hotels and ferries — local platform with best prices

transport: In Bali, renting a scooter (Rp 70,000–100,000/day) is the most practical way to get around — always wear a helmet and carry your international driving licence.
Gojek and Grab ojek (motorbike taxis) are cheap and fast for short distances in cities.
For Java: intercity trains are comfortable and affordable — book at kai.id well in advance for popular routes (Jakarta–Yogyakarta).
Island hopping: use Traveloka or 12Go Asia to compare Pelni ferries, fast boats and domestic flights.
Fast boats between Bali–Gili Islands–Lombok are popular but safety standards vary — choose reputable operators.
Avoid driving a car in Jakarta unless necessary — traffic is extreme.

scams.name: Money changer short-change (Bali)
scams.description: Some private changers in Kuta/Seminyak use sleight of hand to short-change tourists. Use ATMs or the reputable PT Central Kuta changers (no commission, honest rates).

scams.name: Fake police at temples
scams.description: Individuals posing as officials claim you need a 'special permit' or that your clothing is wrong, then demand payment. Ignore them.

scams.name: Taxi at Ngurah Rai airport (Bali)
scams.description: Official taxis outside arrivals charge fixed high rates. Use Grab or pre-booked transfer — Grab is now permitted at many Bali pickup zones.

scams.name: Broken scooter claim
scams.description: Rental shops sometimes claim pre-existing damage when you return the bike. Photograph every scratch before leaving the shop.

scams.name: Ubud 'free tour' to workshops
scams.description: Well-meaning guides offer free walks that inevitably end at silver or batik workshops with pressure to buy.

embassy.phone: Verify official embassy contact before travel
embassy.address: Verify official embassy contact before travel

emergency.notes: In Bali, BIMC Hospital (Kuta) is the recommended international hospital for tourists. Search and rescue: 115.

checklist.item: Passport valid 6+ months beyond intended departure date
Check current VOA status and fee at molina.imigrasi.go.id
Prepare approx. USD 30 cash as backup for Visa on Arrival payment
Purchase travel insurance covering scooter riding and water activities
Book onward/return ticket — immigration may request proof
Download Gojek and Grab apps before arriving
Notify bank of travel and carry a backup card
Buy Airalo eSIM or get a Telkomsel/XL SIM on arrival
Book accommodation in Ubud and Gili Islands early — fills up fast in Jul–Aug
Download offline maps for Bali, Lombok and target islands
Respect dress codes at temples — shoulders and knees must be covered
Check if target volcanoes (Bromo, Rinjani) require permits or guides
